Also introduces Re-add a Member for returning household members on ongoing cases, including its UIM wizard, automatic Application Details creation, access points, eligibility filters, and customization.

children.title: Resolve Outstanding Verifications
children.summary: This section explains how caseworkers resolve outstanding verifications in an integrated case. It notes that evidence not verified by an external system or accepted via client self-attestation must be manually verified, which pauses straight-through processing while the worker contacts the client. It also mentions that verifications are configured as optional to support federal reasonable opportunity/inconsistency periods, allowing program benefits to continue during that time.

children.title: Apply Evidence Changes
children.summary: This section explains how to apply evidence changes in Insurance Affordability integrated cases. After caseworkers add or change evidence, changes must be applied to activate new/updated evidence or remove evidence pending removal. It also covers customizable before/after evidence application processes, and program group logic triggered by evidence activation, which reassesses eligibility and may create, reuse, or reopen product delivery cases. It notes that closure reasons preventing reopening can be configured, including “Created in Error,” and that this logic applies only within Insurance Affordability integrated cases.

children.title: Run Program Group Logic and Activate Product Cases if Required
children.summary: This section explains how Insurance Affordability integrated cases use evidence activation to trigger program group logic and reassessment. It covers applying evidence changes, activating new/updated evidence, and removing pending evidence. It also describes when program group logic creates, reuses, or reopens product delivery cases, including special handling for closed cases and the “Created in Error” closure reason. It notes that closure reasons preventing reopening are configurable and that program group logic rules can be customized to meet project needs.

children.title: Special Enrollments
children.summary: For APTC programs, changes in circumstances can trigger special enrollment outside open enrollment. The system runs special enrollment rules to enable an enrollment link for 60 days from the event’s effective date; at reassessment, caseworkers can manually enable shopping from an IC menu. The list of special enrollment reasons is configurable. The section also covers FFM account transfer behavior for changes of circumstance, including outbound/inbound transfers, default exceptions for Streamlined Medicaid/CHIP, and customizable API mapping/extension points.

children.title: Outbound Account Transfer to Federal Hub
children.summary: For FFM states, this section explains when changes of circumstance on an FFM-originated case must be sent back to the Federal Hub as an outbound account transfer. Transfers occur even without eligibility changes, except when everyone remains only Streamlined Medicaid/CHIP, with exceptions for newly eligible Non-MAGI Medicaid or certain non-MEC programs. It also notes that later FFM-reported changes can trigger an inbound transfer treated by FFM as a change of circumstances but as a new application by the organization.

children.title: Generate Communications
children.summary: This section describes default communications for Change of Circumstance processing in Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance. It covers two client notifications: one for an eligibility decision resulting from a successful evidence change, and one for a preliminary eligibility determination when new evidence creates inconsistencies. It notes that communications are customizable by the organization.

Covers caseworker change-of-circumstance handling in Insurance Affordability integrated cases: viewing the case, recording and applying evidence changes, resolving outstanding verifications, and triggering program group logic/product case updates. Also includes guided evidence wizards (Add a Member, Re-add a Member), special enrollments, FFM outbound/inbound account transfers, coverage start-date configuration, and default client communications. Notes multiple customization points for workflow, rules, evidence mapping, closure reasons, and APIs.

Explains how changes of circumstance are reported and processed in Cúram for Insurance Affordability cases. Covers citizen self-service and caseworker-driven flows, evidence capture and activation, verifications, program group reassessment, product case reuse/reopen rules, special enrollment, adverse-action timing, coverage start-date rules, and FFM outbound/inbound account transfers. Notes customization points for IEG scripts, My Information UI, verification rules, guided wizards (Add/Re-add Member), closure reasons, special enrollment reasons, and account-transfer APIs.

children.title: 2.9 Annual renewals and periodic data matching
Periodic Data Matching
Poll External Systems (PDM)
children.summary: Explains how periodic data matching (PDM) polls external systems and stores returned data as incoming, read-only external evidence on Insurance Affordability integrated cases. Notes organizations must determine timing, target cases/clients, and ensure client authorization. Cúram provides an API to add retrieved evidence and update process control tables; bulk external-system communication is not provided. Default evidence types include MEC, Death Status, Income, and Annual Tax Return, and organizations can configure new external evidence types. Also covers mapping external data into Cúram evidence tables via integration points.

children.title: Map and Store External Evidence
children.summary: This section explains how Cúram handles external evidence for Periodic Data Matching (PDM). It states that organizations must retrieve data from external systems, use the provided API to add it to cases, and map it to Cúram external evidence tables. Default evidence types include Minimum Essential Coverage, Death Status, Income, and Annual Tax Return; organizations may configure new external evidence types. PDM evidence is stored as incoming, read-only evidence on the Insurance Affordability integrated case and tied to the triggering match run.

children.title: Identify Verifications
children.summary: This section explains how to identify and configure verifications using external evidence, including reasonable compatibility checks between client-attested income and trusted data sources. It warns that external evidence should not be referenced directly in eligibility/entitlement rules because it can trigger unwanted redeterminations and performance issues. It also describes projected eligibility: using external data from polling to simulate eligibility outcomes, notify clients via Citizen Account, and record results. Organizations can customize verification rules and extend projected eligibility with additional external evidence types.

children.title: Determining Projected Eligibility
children.summary: Explains how projected eligibility is determined using external evidence from polling/policy data matching, rather than client-attested evidence, to simulate eligibility outcomes. Notes that only the latest polled evidence for the specific run is used, and equivalent client evidence is ignored (example: external Death Status replaces Application Details death info). Also states results are shown in Citizen Account/Citizen Portal and recorded on the case. Mentions organizations can customize projected eligibility to include additional external evidence types via evidence handlers.

children.title: Automatically Process PDM after 30 Days
children.summary: After projected eligibility, cases with no client response are automatically completed after a defined interval, defaulting to 30 days. The batch process updates cases using external evidence converters, and remaining unprocessed cases must be handled by a custom implementation. Organizations must also implement notice generation; Cúram provides no default notice, though sample code and APIs store notices as attachments. This section covers automatic PDM completion, external evidence conversion, and required customization for notices and exception handling.

children.title: Generate Notice
children.summary: This section explains that organizations must implement notice generation for the PDM process. Cúram provides no default notice, and batch processing fails without one. It notes that sample code and APIs can be used to generate and store notices as communications in the Attachments table. The section also links notice generation to client notification timing: clients have 30 days to respond to projected eligibility results before automatic processing or application of updates.

children.title: View PDM Messages and Updates from Polled Data
children.summary: Explains how PDM messages and polled-data updates work after eligibility projection. The client has 30 days to contest externally sourced information; a notification is sent and a Citizen Account message appears even if the polled data matches existing attested data. During the review period, updates can be confirmed by the client or caseworker, then are permanently applied to the integrated case after expiration. This section also sits alongside related PDM tasks such as automatic processing after 30 days and notice generation.

children.title: Contest and Confirm PDM Information Received from External Sources
children.summary: Explains how clients contest periodic data match (PDM) information in Citizen Account. Users see update messages on the home/My Updates pages, can view a projected eligibility notice, and may contest external evidence within 30 days. Contesting opens a modal to confirm the dispute and provide a reason, creates a caseworker task, and removes the update from the list. If no contest is filed, the evidence is automatically applied after the time limit. Also covers configurable messages, custom notices, and contestable evidence types.

children.title: Process PDM Contestation by Caseworker
View PDM notice by caseworker
children.summary: Caseworkers can view PDM notices in the integrated case Communications section, including projected eligibility and evidence changes retrieved from external systems. The section notes that organizations must implement the notice/communication itself; Cúram provides no default notice, and batch processing fails if no notice is generated. It also mentions sample code, API-based notice generation, and storage in the Attachments table. Related nearby content covers viewing PDM on the integrated case and contesting PDM information on behalf of a citizen.

children.title: View PDM on integrated case
children.summary: Explains how caseworkers view PDM data matches in an integrated Insurance Affordability case and what they can do with them. Covers viewing PDM notices in Communications, checking recent transactions, and contesting PDM information on behalf of a citizen. Also notes that contested records become “Caseworker contested,” comments are not shown on the client’s citizen account, and that UIM/customization determines page content while notices must be implemented by the organization.

children.title: Contest and Confirm PDM Information on behalf of a citizen
children.summary: Explains how caseworkers can contest PDM information on behalf of a client from the Insurance Affordability integrated case. The caseworker can enter or edit the contest reason in Data Matches, and the record status changes to Caseworker contested. It notes that these caseworker-generated contests are marked separately and do not appear on the client’s citizen account. The page also sits in a broader PDM workflow covering caseworker resolution, notices, and configurable UIM/customization points.

children.title: Perform reassessment
children.summary: When a caseworker activates evidence, the system triggers existing reassessment processing to create a new determination. This recalculates household eligibility on the Insurance Affordability integrated case and produces new eligibility decisions. The section also notes that program group logic runs when evidence changes are activated, and points to annual renewal processing for QHP, Medicaid, and CHIP, including the ACA requirement for annual QHP renewal.

Explains how a caseworker handles a client’s PDM contestation: a task is created, the caseworker resolves outstanding verifications, updates evidence, and records the contest reason. Also covers configuration/customization of UIM pages and optional task flow. Includes viewing PDM notices in Communications and PDM data matches on the integrated case, noting notices must be implemented by the organization and batch processing fails if no notice is generated. Caseworker-entered contests are marked “Caseworker contested” and do not appear on the client’s citizen account.

Periodic Data Matching (PDM) covers how Cúram polls external sources, maps returned MEC/death/income/tax data to read-only evidence on Insurance Affordability cases, and uses it for verifications and projected eligibility. It explains that organizations must retrieve data, generate notices, configure contestable evidence types, and customize handlers/UIM as needed; Cúram provides APIs but no bulk polling or default notice. The section also describes client/caseworker review flows, 30-day contest periods, and automatic case processing after expiry.

children.title: Annual Renewals
Annual renewals for Qualified Health Plans
Poll External Systems (AR)
children.summary: Explains the annual renewal (AR) step where Cúram polls external systems to retrieve mandatory evidence for QHP renewals, mainly IRS and current income. Describes that the organization must decide timing, cases, clients, and authorization for polling. Polled data is stored as incoming, read-only “External System” evidence on the Insurance Affordability integrated case. The section also notes that this evidence is tied to the renewal run and used with active attested evidence to project eligibility.

children.title: Map and Store External Evidence
children.summary: This section explains how external evidence is handled for Annual Renewals: organizations must retrieve data from external systems and use Cúram’s API to add it to cases, since bulk system communication is not provided. It notes default support for Minimum Essential Coverage, Death Status, Income, and Annual Tax Return evidence, and that incoming tax return data is mapped by default handlers. It also covers mapping external data to evidence tables, using reasonable-compatibility verifications, and warns not to reference external evidence directly in eligibility rules because of redetermination and performance issues.

children.title: Identify Verifications
children.summary: Explains how external evidence is mapped into Cúram and tied to verification rules for annual renewals and projected eligibility. It notes that organizations must retrieve external evidence and use Cúram’s API to add it, since bulk external-system communication is not provided. The section highlights “reasonable compatibility” verifications comparing client-attested income with external Annual Tax Return/Income Details evidence, warns not to reference external evidence directly in eligibility rules, and states that customization is needed for verification rule behavior.

children.title: Determining Projected Eligibility (AR)
children.summary: Explains projected eligibility for Annual Renewals (AR): Cúram runs program group rules using active case data plus external source data to show the effect of applying that data. It notes organizations must retrieve external evidence themselves, use the API to add it to cases, and map external evidence into Cúram tables. Default evidence types include Minimum Essential Coverage, Death Status, Income, and Annual Tax Return. It also warns not to reference external evidence directly in eligibility rules and says reasonable compatibility verification rules are customizable.

children.title: Automatically renew Medicaid or CHIP cases
children.summary: Explains automatic renewal of Medicaid or CHIP cases when all current benefit unit members remain eligible. Describes automatic processing after a defined response interval (default 30 days) for annual renewals and periodic data matching, including use of projected eligibility and polled external evidence. Notes that organizations can customize response days, evidence handlers, and external evidence types, but must build custom processing for cases that cannot be renewed or processed automatically.

children.title: Automatic Processing After Defined Interval
children.summary: Explains automatic case processing after the client response period expires, mainly for periodic data matching and annual renewals. By default, the system runs an automatic completion batch about 30 days after projected eligibility and uses polled external evidence, converted to internal evidence, to redetermine eligibility/entitlement. For QHP annual renewal, if clients do not respond within the interval, polled evidence is applied automatically. Organizations can customize the response-day interval and must handle cases that cannot be processed automatically.

children.title: Generate notice
children.summary: This section explains how to generate the required renewal/eligibility notice for annual renewal processing. It says organizations must implement a custom notice/communication because Cúram provides no default notice, and batch processing will fail if notice generation is not successful. It notes sample code and APIs for generating and storing notices in the Attachments table. It also covers client-facing annual renewal and My Updates messages, projected eligibility notices in Citizen Account PDF form, and what can be customized on renewal pages and evidence displays.

children.title: View updates from polled data and annual renewal messages
children.summary: Explains how annual renewal and polled-data updates are shown to citizens. After eligibility projection, clients see annual renewal messages in Citizen Account and a My Updates page listing renewal data from data matches; annual tax return income is indicated without showing the amount. Projected eligibility notices are available as PDFs in Citizen Account. The section also notes configurable home-page messages, custom My Updates columns/evidence types, and custom projected eligibility notices via batch processes.

children.title: Complete annual renewal for QHP
children.summary: Describes the annual renewal process for Qualified Health Plans (QHP) from the Citizen Account home page: citizens complete the renewal script, review retrieved external data, sign and submit, and the system maps results to the insurance affordability case, potentially auto-renews Medicaid/CHIP, and raises worker tasks. Notes differences between PDM and AR contesting of external information, open-enrollment shopping after submission, configurable response/expiry intervals, and batch-job customization for end-to-end processing. Also covers caseworker-assisted renewals and viewing/creating AR notices.

children.title: Process annual renewals by caseworker
View AR notice
children.summary: Caseworkers can view AR notices in the Communications section of an Insurance Affordability integrated case. These notices show what was sent to the client, including projected eligibility and evidence changes retrieved from external systems. No sample notices are included. The section also notes customization options: the workspace/page containing Communications can be replaced with a custom page, and organizations must develop any required notices.

children.title: View AR on integrated case
children.summary: Explains how caseworkers use the Integrated Case home page to view recent AR transactions, start/sign/submit/complete Insurance Assistance Renewals, and handle outstanding verifications before redetermination. Notes that the case home page and the Sign and Submit action can be customized, and required verifications are configurable. Also describes an advisor that flags client-attested income evidence needing reverification when external renewal data doesn’t match; advice appears on Integrated Case and Evidence Dashboard pages and can be implemented for other evidence types.

children.title: Complete QHP renewal on behalf of a client
children.summary: Caseworkers can complete a QHP annual renewal on a client’s behalf in the caseworker app by using Sign and Submit Insurance Assistance Renewal, which stops the timer and records the client’s confirmation. They must update citizen-reported evidence, resolve required verifications, and later use Complete Insurance Assistance Renewal once outstanding items are finished so a redetermination can be generated. The section also notes configurable verifications and customization of the sign-and-submit action, plus an advisor that flags client-attested income evidence needing reverification after external polling.

children.title: Informing a caseworker when client-attested annual renewal evidence needs reverification
children.summary: Explains how the advisor flags annual renewal income evidence for reverification when a caseworker signs and submits a client’s renewal and external polling creates potentially mismatched evidence. Advice appears on the Integrated Case home page and Evidence Dashboard, and the caseworker must confirm the client-attested evidence is reviewed and verified before the advice disappears. Also notes a sample advisor rule set for income evidence and that similar advice can be implemented for other evidence types.

Explains how caseworkers process annual QHP renewals on behalf of clients: viewing AR notices and recent transactions on an integrated case, using Sign and Submit Insurance Assistance Renewal, resolving outstanding verifications, and completing the renewal so redetermination can be generated. Notes configurable/customizable case pages, verifications, and sign-and-submit action. Also describes an advisor that flags client-attested income evidence needing reverification when external renewal data mismatches, with advice shown on Integrated Case and Evidence Dashboard pages.

Explains the QHP annual renewal flow: Cúram polls external systems for mandatory IRS/current income data, maps/stores it as read-only external evidence, uses it in projected eligibility, and applies reasonable-compatibility verifications. Covers automatic renewal after response deadlines, required custom notice generation, Citizen Account/My Updates messaging, and the client/caseworker paths to complete renewal and handle verifications. Also notes configurable batch jobs, response intervals, evidence handlers, and custom pages/communications.

children.title: Annual Renewals for Medicaid and CHIP
children.summary: Explains annual renewal handling for Medicaid and CHIP cases. Renewal occurs 12 months after the eligibility start date, and successful renewal automatically creates a new product delivery case for the next 12-month certification period. It notes renewals should happen before other case actions, or no new product delivery is created after certification ends. The section also describes custom batch processing for polling external evidence, projected eligibility checks, automatic renewal when eligibility is unchanged, and handling cases that cannot be auto-renewed using PDMRunCaseControlManager and MedicaidProcessAnnualRenewals.

Annual renewals covers QHP, Medicaid, and CHIP renewal flows. It explains polling external systems for mandatory IRS/current income data, storing it as read-only external evidence, using it for projected eligibility and reasonable-compatibility checks, then generating required notices and Citizen Account/My Updates messages. It describes automatic renewal after the response window, client and caseworker renewal completion paths, verification handling, and configurable batch jobs/evidence handlers. Medicaid/CHIP renewals occur 12 months from eligibility start and create a new product delivery case only if renewed before certification ends.

Cúram’s annual renewals and periodic data matching (PDM) section explains scheduled polling of external sources, mapping returned MEC/death/income/tax data into read-only external evidence, using it for verification and projected eligibility, and generating required notices/messages. It covers client and caseworker renewal flows, contestation, 30-day response windows, automatic processing after expiry, and custom batch jobs/handlers/UIM for implementation. It also details Medicaid/CHIP-specific annual renewals: 12-month cycles, automatic new product delivery creation only when renewed before certification ends, and handling cases that can’t auto-renew.

children.title: 2.10 (deprecated) Monitoring Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Applications
children.summary: Deprecated admin reports for monitoring Income Support for Medical Assistance intake processing. Describes how managers can use intake reports to see application counts by state, source, and intervention needed, plus active eligible programs by type. Covers Incoming Applications, Applications Received, Intervention Required, and Eligible programs, with default program abbreviations. Warns that refreshing reports can heavily load the system and disrupt intake processing. Notes limited customization, dependence on curam.intake.use.resilience, and that reports only track applications up to eligibility decision.

Overview of the main Health Care Reform business flows for Income Support for Medical Assistance. It links the role-based flow map to detailed processes covering household eligibility screening, online/navigator/caseworker applications, human services program screening and application, plan cost checking, citizen account maintenance, appeals, change of circumstance, annual renewals/PDM, and deprecated intake monitoring. Includes key configuration/customization points (home page, motivations, IEG scripts, CER rules, PDFs, communications, account pages) and notes some flows may not apply in FFM states.

children.title: 3 Program information
3.1 HCR rate tables
children.summary: Explains HCR rate tables used to maintain rates that change over time and apply during specific effective periods. Notes that rate tables are maintained independently for flexible product pricing. Lists the main HCR rate tables: Annual Federal Poverty Levels for Qualified Health Plan, Annual Federal Poverty Levels for Streamlined Medicaid and CHIP, Tax Filing Thresholds for Dependents, and Tax Filing Thresholds for Dependents for Gross Income. Pages 121–122 are mostly legal notices, copyrights, privacy, and trademarks.

Introduces Health Care Reform (HCR) rate tables for managing time-effective rates used by products. Explains that rate tables are maintained independently for flexible pricing and lists the main tables: Annual Federal Poverty Levels for Qualified Health Plan, Annual Federal Poverty Levels for Streamlined Medicaid and CHIP, Tax Filing Thresholds for Dependents, and Tax Filing Thresholds for Dependents for Gross Income. The remaining pages are legal notices, copyright/license terms, privacy policy, and trademarks.
